reason: S9 evidence has ruled out currently reviewed ylong_http_client-internal production levers as SOTA-scale causes. The strongest remaining causal pointer is ylong_runtime readiness/parking behavior around nested TLS I/O, which is outside gen-000 writable source authority.

requested_writable_source_scope: local pinned ylong_runtime source or an equivalent reviewed dependency patch
authority.rationale: A production-grade fix for the remaining measured gap appears to require runtime scheduler/I/O readiness changes, not another HTTP client micro-optimization.

imports_from_gen_000: all rejected S9 candidate evidence as counterexamples

new_or_refined_steps.transition: Pin and review the exact ylong_runtime source used by the ylong_base benchmark build without changing SOTA target semantics.
Instrument or trace runtime readiness/parking around nested HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy I/O and show a causal gap relative to libcurl/tokio observations.
Implement a behavior-preserving runtime readiness/parking candidate and prove it does not change HTTP/TLS/proxy semantics.
Rebuild and rerun the approved HTTPS proxy benchmark matrix with the runtime candidate, showing hard SOTA metrics and no p95/CPU/RSS regression.
Run final counterexample review only after S9D passes the fixed SOTA thresholds.

forbidden_substitutes: Do not treat current-thread benchmark mode as a production optimization.
Do not treat ylong_http_client-internal hard-negative evidence as S9 pass evidence.
Do not run S10 before S9D passes the fixed thresholds.

review_required.reason: The proposal expands source authority beyond gen-000 writable paths and may require a local dependency patch or upstream runtime change.
